zh.lifeSummary: 當Hannah Root於1699年5月12日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 倫敦勒班農時，她的父親Thomas Root32歲，母親Sarah Clark32歲。她於1718年11月6日與Benoni Clark在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 倫敦勒班農結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和9個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1748年5月10日在美國康乃狄克州劍橋大學默里 倫敦勒班農享年48歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hannah Root于1699年5月12日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 倫敦勒班農时，她的父亲Thomas Root32岁，母亲Sarah Clark32岁。她于1718年11月6日与Benoni Clark在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 倫敦勒班農结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和9个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1748年5月10日在美国康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 倫敦勒班農享年48岁。

nameMeanings.name: Root
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: perhaps a nickname for a cheerful person, from an unrecorded Middle English rote (Old English rōt) ‘glad, cheerful’.

English: from Middle English rote, route, rotte ‘rote’ (of uncertain origin but apparently ultimately akin to Welsh crwth), perhaps used for a player of the medieval stringed instrument, a kind of harp or fiddle. Compare Rutter .
English: perhaps a habitational name from Wroot (Lincolnshire), from Old English wrōt ‘snout, spur of land’.

variants.name: Rooth
meanings: English: habitational name from Rowarth in New Mills (Derbyshire), from Old English rūh ‘rough’ + worth ‘enclosure’.

variants.name: Rutter
meanings: English: occupational name from Old French roteor, roteeur, routeeur ‘player on the rote’, a musical instrument, a kind of harp or fiddle. The regular modern development of the name would have been to Roter (rhyming with boater), and to Router or Rowter (rhyming with doubter). These variants survive in small numbers, but the principal modern form is Rutter, found in fairly large numbers across England, especially in the northeast and in the West Midlands. The shortened vowel in Rutter may have been influenced by rotte, rutte, Middle English variants of Old French rote. Compare Root 2.

English: nickname from Middle English roter, rotour ‘robber, plunderer’, also ‘scoundrel, lecher’, a borrowing of Old French rotier, Anglo-Norman French routier ‘soldier of fortune; robber, highwayman, ruffian’, though this is a less likely source of the modern surname.
Dutch: nickname from Middle Dutch rut(t)er ‘freebooter, footpad’, cognate with 2 above.

variants.name: Rutt
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Root  .
German (Rütt): from a short form of the personal name Rudolf .

variants.name: Roots
meanings: English (Kent): variant of Root , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Hannah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the mother of the prophet Samuel (1 Samuel 1:2), Hebrew Hanna. It is derived from a Hebrew word meaning ‘He (i.e. God) has favoured me (i.e. with a child)’. See also Anne . This form of the name was taken up as a given name by the Puritans in the 16th and 17th centuries and remained popular until the late 19th century. Thereafter it fell somewhat from favour but has enjoyed a massive revival since the 1990s.

variants.name: Hanna
meanings: Either the biblical name ( see Hannah ) or a short form of Johanna .

variants.name: Hana
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Hannah Root was born on 12 May 1699, in Lebanon, New London, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, her father, Thomas Root, was 32 and her mother, Sarah Clark, was 32. She married Benoni Clark on 6 November 1718, in Lebanon, New London, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 9 daughters. She died about 10 May 1748, in Lebanon, New London, Connecticut, United States, at the age of 48.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hannah Root est née le 12 mai 1699, à Lebanon, New London, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, son père, Thomas Root, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Sarah Clark, avait 32 ans. Elle a épousé Benoni Clark le 6 novembre 1718, à Lebanon, New London, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 9 filles. Elle est décédée environ 10 mai 1748, à Lebanon, New London, Connecticut, États-Unis, à l’âge de 48 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hannah Root am 12. Mai 1699 in Lebanon, New London, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas Root 32 und ihre Mutter Sarah Clark 32. Sie hat am 6. November 1718 in Lebanon, New London, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America Benoni Clark geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 9 Töchter. Sie starb um 10. Mai 1748 im Alter von 48 Jahren in Lebanon, New London, Connecticut, Vereinigte Staaten.

ja.lifeSummary: Hannah Rootが1699年5月12日にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ニュー ロンドンレバノンで生まれた時，父Thomas Rootは32歳，母Sarah Clarkは32歳であった。1718年11月6日にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ニュー ロンドンレバノンでBenoni Clarkと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と9人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1748年5月10日にアメリカ合衆国コネチカット州ニュー ロンドンレバノンで48歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Hannah Root(이)가 1699년 5월 12일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 런던 레바넌에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Root(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sarah Clark(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 1718년 11월 6일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 런던 레바넌에서 Benoni Clark(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 아홉 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1748년 5월 10일 미국 코네티컷 주 뉴 런던 레바넌에서 48세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hannah Root nasceu em 12 de maio de 1699, em Lebanon, New London, Conecticute, British Colonial America, seu pai, Thomas Root, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Sarah Clark, tinha 32 anos. Ela casou-se com Benoni Clark em 6 de novembro de 1718, em Lebanon, New London, Conecticute, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 9 filhas. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 10 de maio de 1748, em Lebanon, New London, Conecticute, Estados Unidos da América, com 48 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hannah Root родилась 12 мая 1699 г. в местности лебанон, ню лондон, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика, её отцу по имени Thomas Root было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Sarah Clark, 32. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Benoni Clark 6 ноября 1718 г. в местности лебанон, ню лондон, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 9 дочерей. Она умерла около 10 мая 1748 г. в местности лебанон, ню лондон, Коннектикут, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 48 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hannah Root nació el 12 de mayo de 1699, en Lebanon, New London, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, su papá, Thomas Root, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Sarah Clark, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Benoni Clark el 6 de noviembre de 1718, en Lebanon, New London, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 9 hijas. Murió aproximadamente 10 de mayo de 1748, en Lebanon, New London, Connecticut, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 48 años.
